name: Spanakopita
recipeIngredient: 1 Salt (sea salt preferred)         1/2 lb Feta cheese
1/2 lb Fresh mushrooms, sliced      1 tsp Organo
1 Garlic clove, minced              1/4 tsp Dried rosemary (optional)
Fresh ground pepper                1/4 lb Butter (1 stick)
recipeInstructions:
If using fresh spinach, wash the spinach and remove the rough stems. Place in a large bowl and sprinkle heavily with salt. Rub the salt into the leaves by lifting them up in bunches and rubbing between your hands; the volume of spinach will decrease as you do this. Tear the spinach as you go. Rinse the salt off thoroughly and dry the spinach, squeezing it in bunches in a towel. (If using frozen spinach, let it thaw and squeeze out excess moisture.)
Saute the onion and mushrooms in a little olive oil and butter with garlic and season to taste with salt and pepper until both onions and mushrooms are tender.
Beat the eggs in a large bowl and crumble in the feta cheese. Add the grated Parmesan, then stir in the spinach, onions, and mushrooms. Mix in chopped parsley, Organo, dried rosemary (if using), and additional fresh ground pepper and salt (remembering that the feta is very salty).
